EIA Weekly Petroleum Status Report

Release date: September 25, 2024

U.S. commercial crude oil inventories (excluding those in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve) decreased by 4.5 million barrels from the previous week. At 415.3 million barrels, U.S. crude oil inventories are about 4% below the five year average for this time of year.

WTI crude oil prices rose sharply following this inventory data release, with futures contracts posting significant gains on the day. The surprise draw in crude inventories was larger than analyst expectations of a 1.2 million barrel decrease.

Total motor gasoline inventories decreased by 1.5 million barrels last week and are about 2% below the five year average for this time of year.
